name: Auberge des Aryelets
summary: A small valley restaurant in Aulon focused on local Pyrenees products, better suited to a non-race evening than a tight race-day lunch.
whyItMatters: It gives the Pyrenees segment a real local dinner candidate built around valley products like Noir de Bigorre pork, Pyrenees lamb, trout, and cheeses.
pros: Official site emphasizes local and seasonal valley cooking
Reservation link and phone are published
Good thematic fit for the Tourmalet/Arreau mountain stay
cons: Official page did not expose current opening hours in browsable text
Aulon village access may be inconvenient if roads are busy or closed for race traffic

logistics.opening_hours: Unknown from official page text; verify directly.
logistics.reservation_needed: Yes, recommended; official site links to reservations.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until Arreau/Pyrenees lodging is confirmed; Aulon is near Saint-Lary/Vallee d'Aure.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not suitable for same-day Tourmalet viewing unless access is independently confirmed.
logistics.transit: Likely car/taxi only; verify mountain-road access.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No specific Tour de France closure information found for Aulon; mountain-stage access risk is material.

timing.best_visit_window: Dinner before or after the mountain race day, not during the critical viewing window.
timing.race_day_risk: High for Stage 6 itself; medium on adjacent nights if road access remains busy.

sources.title: Auberge des Aryelets official website
sources.note: Supports address, phone, reservation link, seasonal/local cooking concept, and examples of Pyrenees products used.

open_questions: Verify current opening days/hours and July 2026 reservation availability.
Confirm drive time and road access from the chosen Pyrenees lodging.
